body: Don't let some idiots push you into an eating disorder. You're perfectly fine. This throw around your woman thing sounds so stupid.

Wow… excuse me while I vomit.

	>”she stays at home and does whatever she does”

I am nearly certain that you have *very* little experience taking care of kids, and likely none at all. I’ve worked with kids most of my life, as a gymnastics coach, nanny, after school program facilitator, and as an uncle of quite a few nieces and nephews. Kids are exhausting. Which makes sense given that you’re raising a human being and teaching them to be at least semi-functional in the world. Diaper blowouts, sharpie artwork on the walls, teddy bears in toilets, “*I* wanted to put my socks on!” breakdowns, complaints of being hungry while refusing to eat anything, crying because life is overwhelming for a toddler, and countless other challenges require an incredible amount of energy to navigate. From an evolutionary standpoint, raising offspring is one of the costliest investments.

Regarding your quip about how many times you’ve “read that bullshit,” it is an established statistical fact that women spend more time doing household chores than men. And household chores doesn’t include caring for kids.

You clearly don’t respect women. They don’t “fall into place.” That’s not how any relationship works, which makes me wonder how much experience you have with relationships, much less relationships with women. You come across as a whiny dude who’s bruised butthole about being called out and blames his lack of success with relationships on a notion that men are unfairly treated. It’s very incel-esque. Brb, I have to vomit again.

“Mediocre puss”? Sex is a relational act with another person, not a fleshlight. Your demonstrated penchant for objectifying women speaks volumes about your perception of sex. Pussies are wonderful for people who know how to appreciate and please them. Your attitudes and beliefs undoubtedly exclude you from that category. 

Need to vomit again…

That's true thanks for your advice

Being 17 is not like being 7, lol. You get invested in the relationship and the wounds can be deep. You're saying OP should forgive him and stay friends because "boys will be boys"? Seriously, fuck off. 

The only good thing for OP about being young is that she has all the time she needs to move on and find someone new. But being young doesn't magically make you run in dirt and forget about your problems and the hurt people caused you. 

I'm not saying he was going to be the man of her life, but for fuck's sake, she trusted him and now she might have self esteem and trust issues. Just because he's a "teenage boy" doesn't forgive his mistakes. OP just needed to have a place to talk about her feelings, she didn't need an insensitive asshole to tell her "boys will be boys".

It's unfortunate that you feel that way. I feel bad for your partners. 

This gives me more sympathy for the ex who couldn't ever last long enough during sex. He lasted a bit longer for BJs, but he'd finish within a couple of minutes as soon as he was inside me. I resented it then and we broke up for other reasons, but yikes at least he got hard and was happy to be in my pussy.
